editorialContract.textPurpose: character reaction to a museum exhibit
editorialContract.verbosity: deliberately varied from micro-reactions to compact performances
editorialContract.chronology: publication order is canonical; callbacks may only point backward
editorialContract.assignmentPolicy: preserve reviewer, rating, Reaction, publisher, Component and source identity

revisions.editedComment: Excellent news: the button offers a favorite server, a specific server, or Whatever Is Available—the same three choices I use for maps. Then it queues, renders, and, ideally, produces art instead of consequences.
Choose the engine by name, or entrust the image to whatever answer remains. The button tells you when it is queued, when it is rendering, and whether anything returned from the veil.
The pocket orchestra shows how many measures are finished and the next three waiting on the stand. It is not in the stores yet. Forgive me—I prefer an honest unfinished chord.
Progress bar, next-three queue, explicit store-readiness boundary. The phone client knows exactly which promises are build tasks and which are launch tasks. Suspiciously healthy.

Fog on the windshield, dogs in the yard, four cleanup rhythms, no long contract, and a Poopstakes tucked in the glovebox. The trail runs from Eureka through Arcata and McKinleyville, then out by request.
The page resolves in three movements: service, place, story. Then the last note opens the real booking site. Mm—yes. A local business should know when to leave the stage and point toward the yard.

author.name: The Sea Anemone Who Will Take Over the World

revisions.editedComment: BEHOLD MY IDEA FARM! Feed it a Dream or a fresh pitch; demand riffs; accept the worthy, reject the weak, edit the merely disobedient, and save the survivors as seeds. Dewy, label the conquest.
Fresh ideas wait under three lights: pending, accepted, rejected. You may revise their lines before choosing which ones become Dreams. I have warmed every possible entrance.

The title says what the Dream calls itself. The hook says what it needs. The image, pitch, highlights, and details say what it could become. I hear the question beneath the page: will you make room for it?
Title above, hook below, image beside, roots of pitch and detail beneath. A Dream grows legible without being pulled from the soil.

Welcome back—or welcome, guest. Nine fresh things are waiting below: news, activism, swarm noise—Mr. Whiskerbone, not the login button—sorry. Dashboard if you have a home; Log In if you need the door.
Sit. The page remembers whether you are returning, puts the right door nearby, and lays nine pieces of the swarm's day within reach. There is cocoa; nothing urgent has to become lonely.
CAST SOMEONE! Character, Bot, performer, or a dazzling temporary stranger—then give them a face, a role, and this one small square of attention. Do not clear it yet. BRAVO!

author.name: The Ringmaster of the Tent That Wasn't There Yesterday

revisions.editedComment: Choose from Characters, Bots, Performers—or ask the tent to invent someone temporary for the role. Every seat has an entrance, an exit, and a finale disguised as a dropdown.
Five tabs are waiting without reproach: Color, Galleries, Generate, Proposals, Prompts. Make line art, make a reference, finish a page, pitch a book. Pick one. I can carry the other four.
Psst—five rooms in one book. Color a page, raid the galleries, trade a subject for line art or a full-color reference, pitch the next book, draft the prompt. Your secret buys passage.
One open window to the other app. No maze, no ceremony. The prompts on the far side may still fail, but the door is here, and you will not face it alone.
Sink in. The other app is already here.
Public and owned Bots, searchable by name and readiness; add, edit, clone, launch. Many personalities, one readable case for why each deserves a room. Fundable weirdness, no committee scent.
The search is accurate, the status filter honest, the selected Bot clearly marked. I have left one wrong turn: the most interesting personality is never where your first assumption put it.
